items.title: Each El Nino is unique, says WMO's climate prediction chief
items.summary: GENEVA: Warming El Nino conditions were expected to develop before the end of August, said Wilfran Moufouma Okia, head of climate prediction at the UN's World Meteorological Organisation (WMO), Tuesday. "When El Nino occurs, droughts are expected in West Africa, the Sahel, South Africa, Australia, and southeast Asia," he said. Okia said there was progress, with countries exchanging more information regarding El Nino.

items.title: Fisherman feared drowned while attempting to swim across Pahang river in Temerloh
items.summary: The victim, Samsul Azmi Ishury, 41, was reported missing at about 7.30am, prompting a search and rescue (SAR) operation by the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM), according to an Utusan Malaysi a report today. Temerloh Fire and Rescue Station operations commander PBK II Muhammad Lutfi Burn Abdullah said his team received an emergency call at 7.30am before deploying firefighters to the scene. Rescue operations are ongoing.

items.title: Trump administration proposes 25pct tariff to punish Brazil over trade practices
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration has proposed a new punitive tariff of 25 per cent on many imports from Brazil, after deciding its practices were unfair on a range of issues from digital trade to illegal deforestation, top trade official Jamieson Greer said on Monday. The measures, under the Section 301 trade legislation, cover areas such as electronic payment services, preferential tariffs, intellectual property protection and ethanol market access as well, the Office of the United States Trade Representative said. It proposed the new duties as it released the results of its unfair trade practices investigation into Brazil that started last year under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974.

items.title: Consumers group calls for strong data security in social media age verification rollout
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Muslim Consumers Association (PPIM) has called for strong data security safeguards to be in place for the government's age verification requirement for social media accounts, which came into force yesterday. The association said the policy , which mandates identity verification and restricts access for users under 16, was a crucial step towards strengthening digital safety amid rising online scams, cyber threats and exposure to harmful content. PPIM described the policy as a structural move to balance digital access with user protection.

items.title: Simpang Renggam tragedy: Transport minister demands swift investigation and prosecution over reckless driving deaths
items.summary: Appropriate and firm prosecution action must be taken against those responsible,” he said. Loke also stressed that road safety is a shared responsibility among all road users, warning that a driving licence is not a licence to drive recklessly. “Road safety is a shared responsibility among all road users.

items.title: Decision on calling off 10-day search for missing hiker Jaslinda expected tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A decision on whether to continue or call off the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who has been missing since May 24, is expected tomorrow. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the matter would be decided during a coordination meeting at the Tapah district police headquarters. Earlier, Fire and Rescue Department assistant superintendent II Mohd Hasrizal Kamaruzzaman said clustered clouds, strong winds and dense forest terrain were among the main challenges affecting the SAR operation .

items.title: Johor polls seen as litmus test for BN ahead of GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming Johor state election is shaping up as a key litmus test of Barisan Nasional's (BN) voter support, say political analysts. They said it would also serve as an early gauge of political sentiment towards the unity government ahead of the 16th General Election (GE16). Universiti Sains Malaysia political analyst Professor Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian said the Johor polls would function as both a barometer of BN's current support and a performance assessment of the unity government.

items.title: Chinese citizen suspected of organising drug party at luxury hotel, 51 charged
items.summary: Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the man was among 51 people detained in the operation. He said the investigation also found the event was promoted through Instagram using a private account. "Seventeen other people have been charged under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, while one person was charged under Section 39A of the same Act," he said.

items.title: AMRO revises Asean+3 inflation projection to 1.8pct in 2026
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The Asean+3 Macroeconomic Research Office (AMRO) has revised its inflation projection for Asean+3 to 1.8 per cent from 1.4 per cent in 2026, while maintaining growth estimation at 4.0 per cent. In its latest regional economic outlook report, it said the revised inflation projection was based on the prolonged disruption caused by the Middle East conflict, which has entered its fourth month, exceeding earlier expectations that it would be resolved within two months. AMRO chief economist Dong He said Asean+3 growth has remained resilient, supported by firm domestic demand and technology exports.

items.title: Malaysian families appeal for help over victims trapped in Cambodia, Myanmar scam centres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysian families are appealing for help to rescue their loved ones believed to be trapped in scam compounds in Cambodia and Myanmar, as the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) warns of increasingly sophisticated cross-border job scams. The cases were highlighted at a press conference organised by MHO today. His mother, known only as Ayu, said her son left Malaysia on April 11 after being told he would be working at a shipping port in Russia.

items.title: Man found dead in locked vacant Ipoh building after firefighters cut grille to enter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — A man was found dead inside a vacant two-storey building on Jalan Dato Onn Jaafar in Ipoh, Perak, on Tuesday. He said a team from the Ipoh Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene six minutes later and found the building’s entrance secured with a locked metal grille. Sabarodzi said that upon entering the premises, they found a man on the first floor.

items.title: Johor BN convention likely rescheduled earlier as coalition gears up to contest all 56 seats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) Convention, scheduled for June 14, may be brought forward, according to its secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman. He said the possibility arises from recent political developments in the state, particularly following the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly yesterday. BN will contest all 56 state seats in the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: MIPP says PN machinery already on ground in all 56 Johor seats
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Even before the official dissolution of the state assembly, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) Johor claims it had already activated its election machinery across all 56 state constituencies. Johor MIPP chairman Dr S. Saswin said preparations had been underway months earlier in coordination with Perikatan Nasional (PN) component parties, including Bersatu, Pas and Gerakan. He said MIPP's focus had been on strengthening grassroots structures, improving coordination within PN, and expanding outreach efforts across communities throughout the state.

items.title: 'Goodbye Umi': Mother recalls final moments with daughter killed in Johor crash
items.summary: DUNGUN: A mother has recounted the final moments she shared with her 10-year-old daughter, who was among five people killed in a crash near Simpang Renggam Prison in Johor yesterday. Nurul Ain Mohd Rashid, 38, said her daughter, Nur Airish Syifa Sidek, kissed her cheeks several times before leaving for Johor and smiled as she bid her farewell. "She said, 'Goodbye Umi'.

items.title: Johor election: Muda to defend Puteri Wangsa seat, field more young candidates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Muda will defend the Puteri Wangsa state seat held by its president, Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, in the upcoming Johor state election. Muda added that the 16th Johor state election is not merely about politics or power, but about the future direction of the state and the next generation. The Johor state assembly was dissolved yesterday, paving the way for the state election.

items.title: Scam syndicates shifting operations to Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: International scam syndicates previously operating in Cambodia, Laos and Myanmar are believed to be shifting parts of their operations to Malaysia following intensified crackdowns by regional authorities, according to information obtained by the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO). MHO secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim said the organisation had identified at least two locations in Malaysia believed to be linked to scam syndicate operations. Many workers ended up with almost nothing," he said.

items.title: Police say Chinese national masterminded KL hotel party linked to drug use, supply chain probe
items.summary: Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said investigations found that the event was promoted on Instagram. He said initial investigations showed that the drugs seized were for personal use and shared among party attendees, with no evidence of trafficking. Hussein said that of the 51 individuals detained in the raid, 17 were charged under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, one under Section 39A(1), and 33 under Section 12(2) of the same Act.

items.title: Storm alert: MetMalaysia warns of severe weather in northern, east coast, Sabah and Sarawak until 5pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning covering several states, with heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds expected until late this afternoon. In a Facebook post at 2pm today, MetMalaysia said the weather system is expected to affect Perlis; Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena and Padang Terap); Perak (Kuala Kangsar and Kinta); Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai); Terengganu (Marang, Dungun and Kemaman); and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Bentong and Kuantan). It also said similar conditions are expected in Sarawak, covering Sibu (Selangau), Bintulu, Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi) and Limbang, as well as Sabah, including the Interior (Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), West Coast, Sandakan (Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat.

items.title: Muda to defend Puteri Wangsa seat in Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muda will defend the Puteri Wangsa state seat, currently held by its president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, in the upcoming Johor election. "The 16th Johor state election is not just about a power struggle, but the future of Johor and its next generation. Yesterday, the 56-seat Johor state assembly was dissolved to pave the way for state polls.

items.title: ‘Why Tiram now?’ Marina Ibrahim’s aide challenges DAP’s election strategy ahead of Johor polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — An aide to former Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim has publicly questioned DAP’s decision to reportedly field her in the Tiram state seat for the upcoming Johor election, suggesting the move may have been made hastily due to seat allocation considerations. According to The Star , Ong Huai Yi, who heads Marina’s service centre, challenged Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching’s explanation that Marina was selected because she had the strongest chance of winning in the Malay-majority constituency. In a statement today, Ong questioned what preparations the party had undertaken in Tiram before considering Marina for the seat.

items.title: Thailand dismisses fears over dominant Covid-19 variant
items.summary: BANGKOK: The Thai government has assured the public there is no evidence to suggest the Covid‑19 variant currently circulating in the country is causing more severe illness, while urging continued health precautions and calm. Deputy Government Spokesperson Lalida Persvivatana said the administration, led by Prime Minister and Interior Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, is closely monitoring the situation in coordination with the Ministry of Public Health and relevant agencies. She said the Department of Disease Control (DDC) has identified NB.1.8.1, a sub‑lineage of the JN.1 variant, as the dominant strain in Thailand.

items.title: Two drivers killed in three-vehicle crash in Baling
items.summary: BALING: Two drivers were killed in a crash involving two cars and a bus at Kampung Batu 7, Binjul, Jalan Baru Kulim–Baling–Gerik, here, today. District police chief Superintendent Brandon Richard Joe said police received a report from the public at 6.20am on the accident involving two cars and a bus. He said the crash involved a Perodua Myvi driven by a 23-year-old woman from Pulai, Baling, who was heading towards Kulim, a bus driven by a 47-year-old man travelling in the same direction, and a Proton Saga driven by a 26-year-old man from the opposite direction heading from Kulim towards Gerik.

items.title: Middle East oil output set for 2027 rebound, says BMI
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Oil and gas production across the Middle East is expected to stage a recovery in 2027, as producers move outside their repair and recovery horizons and ramp up their output, according to BMI. The strongest oil production growth next year is expected to come from Iraq at 34.1 per cent year-on-year. Oman is also facing structural production decline.

items.title: After 10 days, decision on continuing search for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin due tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Authorities will decide tomorrow whether to continue or call off the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who disappeared at Gunung Batu Putih on May 24. According to Berita Harian , Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the decision would be made following a coordination meeting involving agencies involved in the operation. “The meeting will discuss the current status and progress of the SAR operation, as well as follow-up actions after 10 days of search efforts,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: KL traffic cops arrest six, four minors, after viral wheelie stunts with pillion daredevils spark outrage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Police have arrested six individuals, including four minors, believed to have been involved in dangerous wheelie stunts on Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Tun Razak and Jalan P. Ramlee here recently. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident was detected after a 44-second video was uploaded to Facebook last Sunday, showing several motorcyclists performing dangerous stunts with pillion riders on public roads. Mohd Zamzuri said the suspects arrested in the second operation had used two blue Yamaha 135LC motorcycles.

items.title: Negri Sembilan PH leaders meet amid talk of state polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN: State Pakatan Harapan leaders and assemblymen held a special meeting at the menteri besar's official residence here this morning, believed to be linked to the state election. Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun's press secretary, Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah, said no press conference is scheduled. Regardless, state Wanita Umno is ready," she said.

items.title: Trump, Netanyahu clash over Lebanon escalation
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel and Hizbollah clashed overnight despite US President Donald Trump's announcement that both sides had agreed to halt fighting ahead of US-hosted talks between Israel and Lebanon on Tuesday. The overnight clashes came after Lebanon said Iran-backed Hizbollah had accepted a US proposal for a "mutual cessation of attacks" after Trump said he persuaded the warring sides to de-escalate. But Hizbollah claimed multiple attacks on Israeli targets, mainly in south Lebanon, including after Trump's announcement.

items.title: Penang unlikely to hold state polls alongside GE, says CM as election timelines diverge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Penang is unlikely to hold its state election simultaneously with the next general election as the gap between the two electoral cycles has become too wide, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. According to Harian Metro , Chow said the state still has about two-and-a-half years remaining in its current term, making it difficult and unfair to dissolve the state assembly prematurely simply to align the state polls with parliamentary elections. Chow was responding to a question on whether Penang would consider holding its next state election together with the general election to reduce costs amid rising living expenses.

items.title: Pentagon designates its press office a classified area
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US Defence Department has designated its press office a classified space, the Pentagon said Monday, making it off-limits to journalists who used to be able to enter the area. "The Pentagon Press Office has been redesignated as a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility" because speechwriters who "routinely handle classified material" work in the area, Acting Pentagon Press Secretary Joel Valdez said in a statement. The Pentagon began enacting new restrictions on journalists soon after President Donald Trump returned to office last year.

items.title: Man arrested over spray attack on couple in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A man has been arrested after allegedly spraying an eye irritant at a married couple in front of a restaurant here yesterday. Seremban district police chief ACP Mohd Yatim Osman said the 50‑year‑old suspect was arrested in front of his house at 11.45pm. "The man then sprayed an eye irritant at the victims, causing irritation and itchiness to their eyes.

items.title: Johor Perikatan finalises 80pc of seat allocation, eyes pacts with MIPP, Muda, IPR
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) has almost completed its seat allocation for the upcoming Johor state election, with about 80 per cent finalised. As announced yesterday, PN will contest all 56 state seats,” he told Bernama when contacted on the coalition’s preparations following the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly. Yesterday, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state legislative assembly after receiving the consent of the Regent of Johor Tunku Mahkota Ismail, paving the way for a state election soon.

items.title: Seven arrested over viral wheelie stunts in KL
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incidents took place at 3.30am on May 23 along Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Tun Razak and Jalan P. Ramlee. "A second operation at 12.54am yesterday led to the arrest of two male riders aged 17 and 16, along with two female pillion riders aged 18 and 15," he said. The second arrest involved two Yamaha 135LC motorcycles.

items.title: France's hard left charts a path to the presidency
items.summary: WHEN Bassi Konate became mayor of Sarcelles this spring, the independent candidate backed by the hard-left France Unbowed (LFI) party ended three decades of municipal rule by the Socialists, the traditional powerhouse of the French left. Konate, who is of Malian heritage, said his election reflected the diversity of modern France. Konate's capture of Sarcelles, where he grew up in social housing, illustrates why LFI has become a formidable force heading into next year's presidential vote.

items.title: Johor voters prioritise cost of living, jobs and stability ahead of state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The rising cost of living, better job opportunities and political stability are among the key concerns of Johoreans ahead of the 16th state election. Former civil servant Nordin Maan, 51, said rising prices of essential goods continued to burden the public, particularly middle- and lower-income groups. "We do not want political disputes that could affect development and public welfare," he said.

items.title: Johor Speaker confirms cancellation of June 22 sitting following Onn Hafiz’s dissolution announcement
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 2 — The special Johor state assembly sitting scheduled for June 22 has been cancelled. The special one-day sitting was cancelled after Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state assembly yesterday. Yesterday, Onn Hafiz announced the dissolution of the state assembly paving the way for a state election.

items.title: Rohingya refugees running businesses, access to funds raises questions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Penang-based non-governmental organisation (NGO) has voiced concern over the growing presence of Rohingya refugees in several parts of the country, claiming the issue has fuelled public unease. "The public sees refugees running businesses, working and driving vehicles. Sophian, who said he regularly visited areas with large Rohingya populations, alleged that some refugees operated businesses using licences held by locals.

items.title: China's yuan hits three-year high, investors eye Mideast peace talks
items.summary: SHANGHAI: China's yuan inched firmer to hit a more than three-year high against the dollar on Tuesday, but gains were capped as investors cautiously evaluated Middle East peace talks. The onshore yuan rose to a high of 6.7621 per dollar in morning trade, the strongest level since February 2023, before changing hands at 6.7625 as of 0315 GMT. Based on Tuesday's official midpoint guidance fix, the yuan's trade-weighted value against its major trading partners, as measured by the CFETS yuan basket index, rose to a near 16-month high of 100.9, according to Reuters calculations based on official data.

items.title: Hanoi curbs kerb culture as city clamps down on pavement vendors
items.summary: HANOI: The pavements of Hanoi's wide boulevards and narrow lanes have long been clogged with bustling food stalls, weaving scooters, and crowds of coffee and beer drinkers sitting on low plastic stools. "Without vendors, I don't think Hanoi is Hanoi anymore," said Nguyen Thi Hoan, 58, who used to sell flowers on the pavement outside a large downtown apartment complex. Street vending is "the tradition of people in Hanoi."

items.title: Trump lashes out at Netanyahu over Lebanon strikes: Report
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump reportedly berated Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a heated phone call over Israel's escalation in Lebanon. Citing US officials and another source on the call, Axios said the US leader called Netanyahu "crazy" and accused him of ingratitude, According to the US news publication, Trump has also directed Israel not to strike Beirut. A news portal said, US officials and sources familiar with the conversation, Trump bluntly told Netanyahu: "You're f***in crazy.

items.title: Indonesia May inflation picks up to 3.08pct y-o-y
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's annual inflation rate quickened to 3.08 per cent in May from 2.42 per cent the previous month, data showed on Tuesday, lifted closer to the top end of the central bank's target range by rising prices of food and transport. Bank Indonesia targets inflation within a range of 1.5 per cent to 3.5 per cent. Core inflation was 2.59 per cent in May, also slightly higher than the poll forecast of 2.52 per cent.

items.title: EasyJet lures takeover interest as investors spot a bargain
items.summary: BRITISH budget airline easyJet has caught the eye of US investment firm Castlelake for a possible takeover. While easyJet has called the timing "highly opportunistic", analysts say its low valuation, slots at key airports and stable fleet make it a prime takeover target as it has struggled to boost its market capitalisation since the Covid-19 pandemic. "This latest bid speculation will likely see a boost again to the easyJet share price."

items.title: Police deploy K9 dogs Rabel and Tyson as rescuers comb Johor plantation for missing 78‑year‑old
items.summary: JEMPOL, June 2 — Two dogs from the Police K9 Unit, Rabel and Tyson, have been deployed to assist in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for an elderly man who has been missing in an oil palm plantation in Palong here since Sunday. Jempol district police chief Supt Norhisham Mustapar in statement said the canines joined the SAR operation which resumed this morning, focusing on a three-kilometre radius from the spot where the victim’s motorcycle was found abandoned in the plantation. The SAR operation is being carried out by personnel from the Fire and Rescue Department, Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), Palong Volunteer Fire Brigade, police personnel and members of the public.

items.title: Man remanded over parang attack on elderly restaurant patron
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An unemployed man who allegedly slashed an elderly restaurant patron with a parang, causing injuries, has been remanded for two days. Magistrate Mustaqim Sukarno allowed the police application to remand the 40-year-old suspect from today to assist in investigations. According to police, the elderly victim was having a drink at a restaurant on Jalan Taman Wijaya in Paloh, Kluang, at 1.30pm last Friday when the suspect approached him.

items.title: Retail weakness to weigh on PetDagang's Q2 performance: CGS International
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Dagangan Bhd's (PetDagang) retail segment earnings before interest and tax (Ebit) is expected to decline quarter-on-quarter (QoQ). PetDagang's commercial Ebit, according to CGS International Securities Sdn Bhd, is projected to rise in the second quarter (Q2 2026), reversing the trend seen in the first quarter. CGS International said this is due to declining Mean of Platts Singapore prices for motor gasoline, diesel and jet fuel.

items.title: Nine-year-old’s 13th-floor fall death in Ampang: Cops record statements from eight including parents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Eight individuals, including the parents of a nine-year-old boy who died after falling from the 13th floor of an apartment in Taman Cahaya Ampang on Sunday, have had their statements recorded to assist investigations. According to Sinar Harian , Ampang Jaya district police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said the investigation is ongoing and the case remains under probe. “So far, eight people have given their statements,” he said when contacted on Tuesday.

items.title: Four family members killed in Johor crash laid to rest in single grave
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 2 — Four family members, including a 10-year-old girl, who were killed in a five-vehicle crash on Jalan Johor Bahru-Ayer Hitam in Simpang Renggam, Johor yesterday, were laid to rest this morning. Mohd Aiman Mohd Rashid, 36; his wife Nor Azlina Abd Latif, 33; her mother Semek Mat Soh, 73; and Mohd Aiman’s niece Nur Airish Syifa Sidek, 10, were buried in a single grave at the Bandar Al-Muktafi Billah Shah Muslim Cemetery here at 9.25am. Earlier, the bodies arrived at Sultan Mahmud Bandar Al-Muktafi Billah Shah Mosque at 8.35am for funeral prayers after being brought from Hospital Enche’ Besar Hajah Khalsom in Kluang, Johor, where post-mortem procedures were conducted last night.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng urges RM150m MSME loans for Chinese and Indian businesses to be channelled through BSN, cites Shariah compliance restrictions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng has called for RM100 million in loans allocated for Chinese-owned micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) and RM50 million under Tekun Nasional for Indian-owned MSMEs to be channelled through Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) instead of other financial institutions. According to a press statement issued in Kuala Lumpur on Tuesday, Lim said placing the special RM100 million financing for Chinese-owned MSMEs under SME Bank and Bank Rakyat — which require Shariah-compliant business activities — has caused dissatisfaction among non-Muslim Chinese businesses. “The RM50 million Skim Pembangunan Usahawan Masyarakat India (SPUMI) under Tekun Nasional for fully owned Indian companies also requires their business activities to be Shariah compliant,” he said.

items.title: Teen Mercedes-Benz driver remanded two days over crash that killed five in Simpang Renggam, including four family members
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 2 — A 19-year-old male teenager has been remanded for two days starting today to assist in investigations into a fatal accident that claimed five lives, including four members of a family, on Jalan Renggam-Simpang Renggam here yesterday. Yesterday, police confirmed the arrest of the local teenager following the horrific accident involving five vehicles. Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh was reported to have said initial investigations found that the suspect was suspected of driving his vehicle recklessly along with a BMW 530e driven by his 22-year-old brother.

items.title: Suspected ‘Rames Gang’ leader charged in Alor Setar court over organised crime membership
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 2 — A man believed to be the leader of the “Rames Gang”, which is alleged to be involved in organised crime activities, was charged at the Sessions Court here today. G. Ramesh, 37, was accused of being a member of the organised criminal group known as the “Rames Gang” in Kampung Sungai Division, Kuala Muda, between January 2024 and December 4, 2025. Previously, the media reported that the suspected gang leader was arrested under Op Tiga on May 22 following cooperation between the Indian and Thai authorities.

items.title: Cops probe Miri woman for allegedly faking armed robbery report involving four men
items.summary: MIRI, June 2 — A 29-year-old woman is under investigation after allegedly lodging a false police report claiming she had been robbed by four armed men in Pujut last week. Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said in a statement on Tuesday that the woman filed the report at 1.58am on May 28, alleging she was robbed while alone near Miri Gospel Church at about 10pm on May 27. However, he added, a comprehensive investigation by Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of Miri District Police Headquarters uncovered multiple inconsistencies in the complainant’s account.

items.title: Johor election will not affect unity govt, says Umno veep Khaled Nordin as ‘era of political dominance’ ends
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 2 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the upcoming Johor election will not affect the dynamics of the unity government, despite the different governments at the state and federal level. Yesterday, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution of the state assembly paving the way for the state election. The previous state election in March 2022 saw BN forming the state government after winning a supermajority of 40 out of 56 contested state constituencies.

items.title: Beruas Umno chief denies slapping Nik Aziz’s grandson, says only ‘touched cheek’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Beruas Umno chief Datuk Azhar Ahmad has denied claims that he slapped a student who is also the grandson of the late Datuk Nik Abdul Aziz Nik Mat during a programme at the Ummah Empowerment Training Institute (ILMU) in Janda Baik, Pahang, on May 20. Online, Azhar said allegations circulating on social media were false and did not reflect what actually happened. He said the interaction occurred openly in front of several witnesses, including UiTM student affairs officers, programme organisers and participants.

items.title: Malaysia eyes Italy, France, US among options after Norway cancels NSM missile deal, says defence minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Malaysia is reviewing several missile manufacturers as alternatives after it failed to secure deliveries of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) following Norway’s cancellation of the export licence for the weapons system. According to Berita Harian , Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said companies under consideration include those from Italy, France, Turkiye, South Korea, the United States and Japan. Mohamed Khaled, who is also Kota Tinggi MP, said the ministry would also evaluate system integration and pricing before making a final decision.

items.title: Eight injured, three seriously, as overloaded Perodua Bezza skids off road in Ranau
items.summary: RANAU, June 2 — Eight people were injured, three seriously, after an overloaded Perodua Bezza skidded off the road in Kampung Kinaratuan on Monday night. Ranau Fire and Rescue chief Rantey Fred said five victims were trapped and extricated by firefighters, while three others escaped before rescuers arrived. Three occupants suffered severe injuries while five, including children, had minor injuries,” he said.

items.title: Woman on way to work among two killed in Baling three-vehicle crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Two people were killed while another was seriously injured in a three-vehicle crash at Batu Tujuh along Jalan Tawar-Kulim near Baling, Kedah, on Tuesday morning. Baling Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Superintendent Zulkhairi Mat Tanjil said another fatality, a man travelling in a Proton Saga, is still being identified. “Upon arrival, we found a crash involving two vehicles, with two fatalities and one seriously injured,” he said.

items.title: Emergency call sparks swift response as Kota Kinabalu firefighters rescue man from streetlight pole
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 2 — Firefighters rescued a man who was found on a streetlight pole in Kampung Sembulan Lama here on Monday. Upon arrival, the team found the individual on the pole and believed to be in distress. Firefighters carried out efforts to persuade the man to come down safely.

items.title: N. Sembilan Pakatan leaders, assemblymen hold morning meeting at MB’s official residence amid dissolution speculation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders and state assembly members in Negri Sembilan held a meeting at the official residence of Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun this morning, amid speculation that it could be linked to the possible dissolution of the state legislative assembly. Menteri Besar press secretary Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah, however, said no press conference was scheduled following the meeting. The development comes amid growing political reactions and calls for Negri Sembilan to follow Johor’s move in dissolving its state assembly yesterday.

items.title: Malaysia condemns Zionist settlers’ storming of Al‑Aqsa Mosque, calls desecration abhorrent and provocative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Malaysia has expressed utter condemnation for the latest storming of the Al-Aqsa Mosque by illegal Zionist Israeli settlers, including the raising of the Zionist Israeli flag within the holy compound. The ministry, also known as Wisma Putra, said the recent action by the settlers, supported by the Zionist Israeli regime, is an attempt to change the reality on the ground as well as the cultural and historical characteristics of the Holy Al Aqsa. Malaysia reiterated its calls for the international community and the United Nations (UN) to take immediate and concrete action to halt these violations and ensure full accountability of the Zionist Israeli regime.

items.title: Cops arrest man after alleged pepper spray attack on couple in Seremban
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — A 50-year-old man has been arrested less than six hours after he allegedly sprayed a couple with a pepper spray-like substance in Seremban 2 yesterday. Online, Seremban district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said the suspect was detained at about 11.45pm in front of his home in Seremban following a report lodged by the 49-year-old female victim. Police also seized a black pepper spray canister bearing the word “Police” believed to have been used in the incident.

items.title: Cops probe LDP three-car crash in Petaling Jaya allegedly caused by wrong-way driver
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 2 — Police are investigating a man who allegedly drove against traffic and caused a three-vehicle collision along Kilometer 24 of the Damansara–Puchong Expressway (LDP) on Sunday. Online, Serdang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Muhamad Farid Ahmad said authorities received a report on the incident at about 1.07pm. He said the Honda City struck a Perodua Axia and a Honda CR-V, which were travelling from Sunway towards Puchong.
